metadata.title: States PCS: Arunachal Pradesh GK Questions for APPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which tribe in Arunachal Pradesh is known for practicing hanging burials?
questions.explanation: The Apatani tribe is known for unique hanging burials, combining animist traditions and ancestor veneration in Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.question: Which is the only river in Arunachal Pradesh that flows northwards into Tibet?
questions.explanation: The Siang, known as Tsangpo in Tibet and Brahmaputra in Assam, is the only Arunachal river that flows north into Tibet before looping south.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain passes in Arunachal Pradesh connects India and Tibet?
questions.explanation: Bum La Pass, near Tawang in Arunachal Pradesh, links India and Tibet and was notable during the 1962 Indo-China war.

questions.question: Which community in Arunachal Pradesh is renowned for brewing Apong, a traditional rice beer?
questions.explanation: Apong is a traditional rice beer primarily brewed by the Adi community, often served during festivals and rituals in Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.question: Which wildlife sanctuary in Arunachal Pradesh is being considered for UNESCO World Heritage status?
questions.explanation: Dihang-Dibang Biosphere Reserve harbors species like red panda and Mishmi takin, and is proposed as a UNESCO site for its unique Eastern Himalayan biodiversity.

questions.question: How many countries share an international border with Arunachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh borders three countries: China (north), Bhutan (west), and Myanmar (east).

questions.question: Which of the following is the official state bird and state flower of Arunachal Pradesh?

options.label: A
options.text: State Bird: Black Bulbul; State Flower: Blue Vanda Orchid

options.label: B
options.text: State Bird: Great Hornbill; State Flower: Foxtail Orchid

options.label: C
options.text: State Bird: Blyth’s Tragopan; State Flower: Red Rhododendron

options.label: D
options.text: State Bird: White-bellied Heron; State Flower: Siroi Lily

answer.label: B
answer.text: State Bird: Great Hornbill; State Flower: Foxtail Orchid

questions.explanation: The Great Hornbill and Foxtail Orchid are the designated state bird and flower of Arunachal Pradesh, representing the state’s rich biodiversity.

questions.question: Which festival is celebrated by the Galo tribe to mark their New Year?
questions.explanation: Mopin is the Galo New Year and harvest festival in Arunachal Pradesh, featuring rituals for prosperity, Popir dance, and rice wine Apong.

questions.question: Which national park in Arunachal Pradesh is renowned for housing both the red panda and the Mishmi takin?
questions.explanation: Namdapha National Park, in eastern Arunachal Pradesh, is known for its rich biodiversity, notably supporting the rare red panda and Mishmi takin.

questions.question: Which is the largest monastery located in Arunachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Tawang Monastery, founded in the 17th century, is the largest in Arunachal Pradesh and India, and is a major center of Mahayana Buddhism.
